questions.stem: A psychologist is evaluating a 10-year-old boy referred for academic difficulties. Testing reveals average intelligence but significant reading deficits. Which of the following is MOST accurate regarding specific learning disorder with impairment in reading?
options: It is diagnosed when IQ scores fall significantly below average
It represents approximately 80% of all specific learning disorder cases
ADHD is rarely comorbid with specific learning disorders
It is equally common in boys and girls and typically resolves by adolescence
questions.answer: It represents approximately 80% of all specific learning disorder cases
questions.explanation: Approximately 80% of children with specific learning disorders have a reading disorder, making it by far the most common subtype. Option A is incorrect because people with specific learning disorders typically have average to above-average IQ; that discrepancy is part of what distinguishes learning disorders from intellectual disability. Option C is wrong because ADHD is actually the most common comorbid psychiatric disorder with specific learning disorders. Option D is misleading; while prevalence rates may be similar across genders in some studies, learning disorders tend to be chronic conditions that persist into adulthood rather than resolving by adolescence.
questions.kn_explanation: Reading deficiency in a child despite average intelligence suggests a specific learning disorder (e.g., dyslexia), which is a disorder that impacts the expected course of development over the lifespan.
questions.quality_comment: Tests key epidemiological facts about reading disorders. The lesson covers prevalence and comorbidity well but long-term prognosis is
